title: oxymeris rufescens
intro: Oxymeris rufescens, also known as the "red wood", is a type of tree or shrub native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Oxymeris rufescens lie in its bark, which contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. The herbal preparations of Oxymeris rufescens typically involve the extraction of its bioactive compounds through methods such as decoction or infusion, which can be used to create teas, tonics, or ointments for medicinal purposes.
